message:
Implement stale issue/PR detection in GitHub collector and fix vc_config clippy lints

The GitHub collector previously hardcoded stale_issues_30d and stale_prs_7d to 0
with TODO comments, meaning the triage summary never reported stale items. This
commit implements proper timestamp-based staleness detection so that operators
get accurate triage data about aging issues (>30 days) and pull requests (>7 days).

Changes to crates/vc_collect/src/collectors/github.rs:
- Add parse_github_timestamp() to safely parse RFC 3339 timestamps from the
  GitHub API, returning None on malformed or missing values rather than panicking.
- Add last_activity_at() which prefers updated_at but falls back to created_at,
  ensuring graceful degradation when GitHub returns partial data.
- Add is_stale() which computes age relative to a given "now" timestamp, using
  saturating arithmetic to avoid overflow on far-future or far-past dates.
- Refactor create_triage_summary() to delegate to create_triage_summary_at()
  which accepts an explicit "now" parameter. This makes the logic deterministic
  and fully testable without mocking system time.
- Replace the hardcoded zeros with actual filter chains that count open issues
  not updated in 30 days and open PRs not updated in 7 days.
- Add test_triage_summary_stale_detection covering: stale items with valid
  updated_at, stale items falling back to created_at when updated_at is
  malformed, recent items that should not be stale, items with completely
  invalid/missing timestamps (should not count as stale), and closed items
  that should be excluded regardless of age.
- Update existing test_triage_summary to use a fixed "now" timestamp and
  assert that stale counts are 0 for recently-active items.

Changes to crates/vc_config/src/lib.rs:
- Fix clippy doc_markdown warnings by wrapping quoted config paths in backticks
  within doc comments (e.g., "machines.orko.ssh_user" -> `"machines.orko.ssh_user"`).
- Add #[must_use] to with_suggestion(), new(), has_errors(), has_issues(),
  lint(), and generate_default_toml() to satisfy clippy must_use_candidate and
  return_self_not_must_use lints. These methods return values that callers
  should never silently discard.
- Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] to lint() since its length is inherent
  to the number of lint checks it performs and splitting it would hurt readability.
- Change test assertion from Duration::from_secs(120) to Duration::from_mins(2)
  for clarity -- same value, more readable intent.

Changes to .beads/issues.jsonl:
- Add issue bd-1jm (closed) tracking the stale detection bug fix above.
- Add issue bd-apm (in_progress) tracking the vc_config clippy lint fixes.
- Close issue bd-new (Phase 7 epic) and bd-new.4 (digest reports) as done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15a","title":"Implement audit trail system","description":"Implement comprehensive audit trail system.

PURPOSE:
Track all actions taken by vc, collectors, autopilot, and users for accountability, debugging, and compliance.

DATA MODEL (from plan Section 9.4):
CREATE TABLE audit_events(
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,  -- collector_run, autopilot_action, user_command, guardian_action
    actor TEXT,       -- collector name, autopilot, user, guardian
    machine_id TEXT,
    action TEXT,
    result TEXT,      -- success, failure, skipped
    details_json TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_ts ON audit_events(ts);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_type ON audit_events(event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_machine ON audit_events(machine_id);

AUDIT EVENT TYPES:

1. COLLECTOR RUNS
- Collector name, machine_id, start/end time, duration
- Bytes parsed, rows inserted, errors/warnings
- Cursor state changes

2. AUTOPILOT EXECUTIONS
- Exact command run
- Machine_id
- User confirmation state (if applicable)
- Result exit code
- Before/after state summary

3. USER COMMANDS
- All vc CLI invocations
- TUI/web interactions that trigger actions
- Parameters and results

4. GUARDIAN ACTIONS
- Playbook executions
- Individual step results
- Rollback events

IMPLEMENTATION:
pub struct AuditEvent {
    pub ts: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub event_type: AuditEventType,
    pub actor: String,
    pub machine_id: Option<String>,
    pub action: String,
    pub result: AuditResult,
    pub details: serde_json::Value,
}

pub trait Auditable {
    fn to_audit_event(&self) -> AuditEvent;
}

// Implement for all actionable types
impl Auditable for CollectorRun { ... }
impl Auditable for AutopilotAction { ... }
impl Auditable for GuardianStep { ... }

CLI COMMANDS:
vc audit list                        # Recent audit events
vc audit list --type guardian        # Filter by type
vc audit list --machine orko         # Filter by machine
vc audit list --since 24h            # Time filter
vc audit show <event_id>             # Full details

RETENTION:
- Keep detailed audit for 90 days
- Aggregate to daily summaries forever

TESTS:
- Test audit event creation for each type
- Test filtering and searching
- Test retention/aggregation

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for audit event normalization, enum mapping, and JSON serialization.
- Integration tests that verify collector runs, alert triggers, and guardian actions emit audit_events rows with correct details.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_audit_trail.sh` exercising poll → alert → guardian → cli actions; assert row counts and field integrity.
- Structured logs and JSON summary in `tests/logs/` including event counts by type and failure reasons.

 {"id":"bd-17d","title":"Implement ntm (Named Tmux Manager) collector","description":"Implement collector for ntm (Named Tmux Manager) - the nerve center of agent orchestration.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- Shell out to ntm --robot-status and ntm --robot-list for JSON output
- Optionally parse events.jsonl if ntm provides it
- Use CLI Snapshot pattern (stateless)

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE ntm_sessions_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    session_name TEXT,
    work_dir TEXT,
    git_branch TEXT,
    agent_counts_json TEXT,
    panes_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, session_name)
);

CREATE TABLE ntm_activity_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    total_agents INTEGER,
    by_type_json TEXT,
    by_state_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE ntm_events(
    machine_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,
    session_name TEXT,
    agent_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement Collector trait for NtmCollector
- Handle missing ntm binary gracefully (fail-soft)
- Parse JSON output from ntm --robot-status
- Extract session details: name, work_dir, branch, agent counts
- Store raw_json for forward compatibility

UI VALUE:
- Agent fleet map by machine/repo/session
- Real-time view of which agents are working where
- Session health monitoring

TESTS:
- Mock ntm CLI outputs
- Verify table population
- Test missing binary handling

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-1h6","title":"Implement health score calculation system","description":"Implement the health score calculation system in vc_query crate.

PURPOSE:
Compute health scores for machines and the overall fleet based on multiple factors. This is the core intelligence that powers the at a glance view.

DATA MODEL (from plan):
CREATE TABLE health_factors(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    factor_id TEXT,
    severity TEXT,
    score REAL,
    details_json TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE health_summary(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_score REAL,
    worst_factor_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

HEALTH FACTORS TO COMPUTE:
1. System resources (CPU, memory, disk, load)
2. Agent velocity (tokens/min, stuck detection)
3. Rate limit headroom (time to limit)
4. DCG denies (frequency and severity)
5. Network anomalies (unknown providers)
6. RCH queue depth
7. Mail backlog (urgent unread)
8. Repo cleanliness (dirty count)
9. Process issues (zombies, stuck)
10. Data freshness + drift (from collector_health/drift_events)

SCORING ALGORITHM:
- Weighted average with worst-factor penalty
- Each factor has weight and score (0.0=critical to 1.0=healthy)
- Apply penalty for critical factors (each critical = -0.1)
- Penalize stale data (freshness_seconds over threshold reduces score)
- Final score clamped to [0.0, 1.0]

THRESHOLDS (configurable):
- Disk: less than 10% free = critical, less than 20% = warning
- CPU: greater than 90% for 5m = critical, greater than 75% for 5m = warning
- Load: greater than 2x cores = critical, greater than 1.5x cores = warning
- Rate limit: less than 10min to limit = critical, less than 30min = warning
- Agent velocity: less than 10 tok/m for 15m = critical
- Freshness: collector stale beyond threshold reduces score

INTEGRATION:
- Called after each poll cycle
- Results stored in health_factors and health_summary tables
- Used by TUI overview, web dashboard, alerts, autopilot

TESTS:
- Test factor computation for each metric type
- Test weighted scoring algorithm
- Test threshold classification
- Test fleet-wide aggregation

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for factor weights, thresholds, penalty rules, and score clamping.
- Fixture-based tests for multi-factor scenarios (disk critical + mail backlog + dcg spikes).
- Integration tests: insert synthetic data into DuckDB and validate health_summary outputs.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_health_scores.sh` to verify score behavior under controlled inputs.
- Structured logs: factors, weights, penalties, final_score,  {"id":"bd-1ly","title":"Implement fleet orchestration commands","description":"Implement fleet orchestration commands for advanced agent management.

PURPOSE:
Provide high-level commands for scaling, load balancing, migration, and emergency operations across the agent fleet.

FLEET COMMANDS (from plan Section 6.3):

1. SPAWN AGENTS
vc fleet spawn --type claude --count 5 --machine gpu-box --model opus
- Spawns new agent instances on target machine
- Validates machine capacity before spawning
- Respects rate limits and quotas

2. REBALANCE FLEET
vc fleet rebalance --strategy even-load --preserve-locality
Strategies:
- even-load: Distribute by CPU/memory
- even-agents: Equal agent counts
- cost-optimized: Prefer cheaper machines
- latency-optimized: Keep agents near their repos

3. EMERGENCY STOP
vc fleet emergency-stop --scope machine:orko --reason "maintenance"
Scopes: machine, provider, all, agent-type
- Graceful shutdown with state preservation
- Requires confirmation unless --force
- Logs to audit trail

4. MIGRATE WORKLOAD
vc fleet migrate --from orko --to sydneymc --workload "repo:smartedgar*"
- Moves active workloads between machines
- Supports glob patterns for workload selection
- Tracks migration progress

5. CANARY DEPLOY
vc fleet canary --config new_prompt_v2.toml --percentage 10 --auto-rollback
- Deploy new config to subset of agents
- Monitor for regressions
- Auto-rollback if metrics degrade

DATA MODEL:
CREATE TABLE fleet_commands(
    command_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    command_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    params_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    completed_at TIMESTAMP,
    result_json TEXT,
    error_message TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE fleet_migrations(
    migration_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    from_machine TEXT,
    to_machine TEXT,
    workload_spec TEXT,
    status TEXT,
    agents_migrated INTEGER,
    agents_total INTEGER,
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    completed_at TIMESTAMP
);

IMPLEMENTATION:
- vc_guardian crate handles execution
- Integrate with ntm for agent spawning/stopping
- Validate machine capabilities before operations
- Full audit logging

TESTS:
- Test dry-run mode for all commands
- Test emergency stop with mock agents
- Test rebalance strategy calculations
- Test canary rollback triggers

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for command validation, capacity checks, and safety confirmations (including --dry-run).
- Integration tests with SSH runner mocks and audit trail verification.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_fleet_commands.sh` using localhost/mocks; verify no destructive actions without explicit flags; JSON summary + audit events.

 {"id":"bd-264","title":"Implement retention policy and vc vacuum command","description":"Implement retention policy system and vc vacuum command.

PURPOSE:
Manage data lifecycle to prevent unbounded storage growth while preserving critical history.

RETENTION POLICY (from plan Section 5.3):
| Data Type | Full Resolution | Aggregates |
|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| System samples | 7-30 days | Forever |
| Event logs (dcg, rano) | 90 days raw | Forever |
| Snapshots | 30 days | Forever |
| Derived rollups | N/A | Forever |

DUCKDB SCHEMA:
CREATE TABLE retention_policies(
    policy_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    table_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    retention_days INTEGER NOT NULL,
    aggregate_table TEXT,
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    last_vacuum_at TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE retention_log(
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    policy_id TEXT,
    table_name TEXT,
    rows_deleted BIGINT,
    rows_aggregated BIGINT,
    duration_ms BIGINT
);

CLI COMMANDS:
vc vacuum                    # Run all retention policies
vc vacuum --dry-run          # Show what would be deleted
vc vacuum --table sys_samples  # Run for specific table
vc retention list            # Show all policies
vc retention set --table sys_samples --days 14

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Before deleting: aggregate data to rollup tables
2. Use transactions for safety
3. Log all deletions to retention_log
4. Respect no deletion discipline - make retention OPT-IN and transparent
5. Support Parquet export before truncation

AGGREGATION EXAMPLES:
- sys_samples: aggregate to hourly/daily averages
- dcg_events: aggregate to daily counts by severity
- repo_status_snapshots: keep only latest per day

SAFETY:
- Never delete without explicit user configuration
- Show preview before deletion
- Maintain audit trail
- Support recovery via Parquet archives

TESTS:
- Test dry-run mode
- Test aggregation correctness
- Test retention_log entries
- Test Parquet export/archive

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for policy parsing, retention math, and dry-run output formatting.
- Integration tests with temporary DuckDB: aggregate-then-delete workflow, rollback on failure.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_retention_vacuum.sh` verifying opt-in behavior, log entries, and Parquet export; JSON summary in tests/logs/.

 {"id":"bd-3nb.5","title":"Implement TUI machine drill-down views","description":"## Overview
Add TUI screens for machine inventory, individual machine details, and remote data visualization.

## Background
With machine registry, SSH runner, and remote collectors in place, users need visual interfaces to monitor their fleet of machines and drill down into individual machine health and data.

## Screens to Implement

### 1. Machines Overview Screen
```rust
pub struct MachinesScreen {
    machines: Vec<MachineRow>,
    selected: usize,
    sort_col: MachineSort,
}

pub struct MachineRow {
    machine_id: String,
    hostname: String,
    status: MachineStatus,      // Online, Offline, Unknown
    tool_count: usize,          // Number of available tools
    last_seen: DateTime<Utc>,
    last_probe: DateTime<Utc>,
    tags: Vec<String>,
}

// Visual layout:
// ╭─ Machines ─────────────────────────────────────────╮
// │ ID          Hostname        Status  Tools  Last   │
// │ ─────────── ─────────────── ─────── ────── ────── │
// │ local       devbox          ● 🟢     12    now    │
// │ mac-mini-1  192.168.1.100   ● 🟢     10    2m ago │
// │ mac-mini-2  192.168.1.101   ● 🔴     --    1h ago │
// │ hetzner-1   hetzner1.ex...  ● 🟢      8    5m ago │
// ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### 2. Machine Detail Screen
```rust
pub struct MachineDetailScreen {
    machine: Machine,
    tools: Vec<ToolInfo>,
    recent_collections: Vec<CollectionEvent>,
    system_stats: Option<SystemStats>,  // If sysmoni available
}

// Visual layout:
// ╭─ mac-mini-1 ───────────────────────────────────────╮
// │ Hostname: 192.168.1.100    Status: 🟢 Online       │
// │ SSH: ubuntu@192.168.1.100  Tags: [mini, builder]   │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
// │ Available Tools:                                   │
// │   caut v0.3.2    ntm v0.5.1     rch v0.2.0        │
// │   ru v0.4.0      sysmoni v0.1.0                   │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
// │ System Stats (via sysmoni):                        │
// │   CPU: ████████░░ 78%    Memory: ██████░░░░ 62%   │
// │   Load: 2.4 / 4.0        Disk: ███░░░░░░░ 34%     │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

// │   ntm     5m ago   12 records    0.1s             │
// │   rch     5m ago   156 records   0.5s             │
// ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### 3. Cross-Machine Comparison View
```rust
pub struct MachineCompareScreen {
    machines: Vec<String>,      // Selected machines to compare
    metric: CompareMetric,      // CPU, Memory, Build Times, etc.
    time_range: TimeRange,
}

// Visual: Side-by-side sparklines or bar charts
// comparing same metric across selected machines
```

## Key Interactions
- `m` to open machines screen
- `Enter` on a machine to drill into details
- `p` to trigger probe on selected machine
- `c` to enter compare mode (multi-select)
- `r` to refresh / re-collect data
- `t` to filter by tags

## Data Queries
```sql
-- Machine overview
SELECT 
    m.machine_id, m.hostname, m.status,
    COUNT(mt.tool_name) as tool_count,
    m.last_seen_at, m.last_probe_at,
    m.tags
FROM machines m
LEFT JOIN machine_tools mt ON m.machine_id = mt.machine_id AND mt.is_available
GROUP BY m.machine_id;

-- Machine recent collections
SELECT 
    tool_name,
    collected_at,
    record_count,
    duration_ms
FROM collection_log
WHERE machine_id = ?
ORDER BY collected_at DESC
LIMIT 10;

-- Cross-machine comparison (e.g., rch build times)
SELECT 
    machine_id,
    AVG(duration_ms) as avg_build_time,
    COUNT(*) as build_count
FROM rch_compilations
WHERE collected_at > now() - INTERVAL 1 DAY
GROUP BY machine_id;
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Machines overview screen shows all registered machines
- [ ] Status icons indicate online/offline state
- [ ] Machine detail shows tools, stats, recent collections
- [ ] Drill-down navigation from overview to detail
- [ ] Cross-machine comparison for key metrics
- [ ] Tag filtering works on machines list
- [ ] Probe action triggerable from TUI

## Testing & Logging
- View-model unit tests for filtering, sorting, and state transitions.
- Render snapshot tests using ratatui TestBackend for key layouts.
- Navigation/keybinding tests (tab/enter/filter/search).
- E2E: tests/e2e/tui/test_<screen>.sh with expect/pexpect; capture logs and screenshots on failure.

 {"id":"bd-new.1","title":"Implement collector health + freshness + drift detection","description":"## Background
vc health and alerts are only trustworthy if we can quantify data freshness and detect drift from expected baselines. The long-form plans emphasize “stale data” badges and incident replay; this bead establishes the data-quality layer that all higher-level reasoning depends on.

## Goals
- Track per-collector freshness and success rate per machine.
- Detect baseline drift for machine profiles (CPU/mem/disk/net) and collector payloads.
- Surface data-quality signals to health score, alerts, and UI/robot outputs.
- Record provenance/lineage metadata to make explanations auditable.

## Implementation
### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE collector_health(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collector TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    success BOOLEAN,
    duration_ms BIGINT,
    rows_inserted BIGINT,
    bytes_parsed BIGINT,
    error_class TEXT,
    freshness_seconds BIGINT,
    payload_hash TEXT,
    collector_version TEXT,
    schema_version TEXT,
    cursor_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collector, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE machine_baselines(
    machine_id TEXT,
    baseline_window TEXT,       -- e.g. "7d" or "30d"
    computed_at TIMESTAMP,
    metrics_json TEXT,          -- mean/std/percentiles per metric
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, baseline_window)
);

CREATE TABLE drift_events(
    machine_id TEXT,
    detected_at TIMESTAMP,
    metric TEXT,
    current_value REAL,
    baseline_mean REAL,
    baseline_std REAL,
    z_score REAL,
    severity TEXT,
    evidence_json TEXT
);
```

### Logic
- Update `collector_health` from audit_events + collector runtime metadata.
- Freshness = now - last_successful_collect for that collector/machine.
- Baseline drift detection: rolling z-score or percentile divergence for core sys metrics.
- Emit `drift_events` when z-score exceeds configurable threshold.
- Add vc.toml config for freshness thresholds and drift sensitivity.

### Outputs
- Add staleness + drift summaries into health score inputs.
- Surface to TUI overview + robot health output as warnings.

## Risks
- False positives on drift during planned load spikes.
- Overhead from baseline computation if window too large.

## Success Criteria
- Staleness is visible in robot output and UI for each collector.
- Drift events are recorded and appear in incident evidence.
- Health score incorporates data freshness weighting.
- Lineage metadata allows tracing any alert back to inputs.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for freshness calculation and baseline stats (fixed seeds).
- Golden-data tests for drift detection on known time series.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_data_quality.sh` with JSON summary and log artifacts.
- Structured logs: collector, machine_id, freshness_seconds, drift_count.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T00:25:18.018868033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T00:25:18.018634042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:11.254659491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.106694788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.10","title":"Implement config lint + wizard","description":"## Background
vc.toml will grow in complexity. Operators need validation, linting, and a guided wizard to prevent misconfiguration.

## Goals
- `vc config lint` that validates machines, SSH targets, and collector settings.
- `vc config wizard` to generate minimal config interactively or from templates.
- Emit actionable error messages with fixes.
- Provide non‑destructive diff preview when generating changes.

## Implementation
- Add JSON schema for config + custom validators (SSH, paths, tokens).
- Support `--fix` suggestions (non‑destructive).
- Validate machine tags and tool enablement coherence.
- Wizard writes to new file unless `--overwrite` is explicitly set.

## Risks
- Wizard must not overwrite existing config without explicit user action.

## Success Criteria
- Lint catches invalid configs and suggests fixes.
- Wizard can bootstrap a valid config from scratch.
- Diff preview shows exact changes before write.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for validators + schema rules.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/config/test_config_lint.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:54.133716478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:12:53.833369347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:12:53.833317158Z","close_reason":"Implemented config lint + wizard with:
- LintSeverity, LintIssue, LintSuggestion, LintResult types in vc_config
- lint() method on VcConfig with 15+ validation rules (errors, warnings, info)
- generate_default_toml() for config generation
- to_toml() for serialization

 {"id":"bd-new.11","title":"Implement query guardrails + safe templates","description":"## Background
`vc query` is powerful but dangerous if used freely by agents. Guardrails and safe templates prevent expensive or unsafe queries.

## Goals
- Add a read‑only query sandbox with allowlisted templates.
- Enforce query timeouts + row limits.
- Provide explainability (why query allowed/denied).
- Ensure queries execute on a read‑only connection.

## Implementation
- `vc query --template <name> --params ...` for safe presets.

- Add max runtime and max output rows/bytes.

- Templates cover 80% of typical use.

 {"id":"bd-new.2","title":"Implement adaptive polling + on-demand profiling","description":"## Background
Static poll intervals are either too expensive or too slow. We need adaptive scheduling and on-demand high-frequency profiling to react to incidents without permanent overhead.

## Goals
- Adaptive poll scheduler that increases frequency for “hot” machines/collectors.
- On-demand profiling bursts triggered by alerts or manual commands.
- Ensure bounded concurrency and global rate limits still respected.
- Quarantine/backoff for failing collectors to avoid thrash.

## Implementation
### Adaptive Scheduler
- Use collector_health freshness + alert severity to compute per-machine poll intervals.
- Policy: min/max interval (e.g., 15s–5m), exponential backoff on repeated failures.
- Quarantine collectors after N consecutive failures; require manual or timed reset.
- Store decisions in `poll_schedule_decisions` for auditability.
- Configurable per-collector overrides in vc.toml (pin interval, disable adaptive).

### On-Demand Profiling
- New command: `vc profile --machine <id> --duration 120 --interval 2s`.
- Temporarily switch sysmoni to high-frequency mode and capture snapshots.
- Write to `sys_profile_samples` table and link to alert/incident IDs.

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE poll_schedule_decisions(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collector TEXT,
    decided_at TIMESTAMP,
    next_interval_seconds INTEGER,
    reason_json TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE sys_profile_samples(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    profile_id TEXT,
    metrics_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT
);
```

## Risks
- Over-aggressive polling can overload remote machines.
- Profiling bursts must be rate-limited and opt-in.

## Success Criteria
- Poll intervals adjust based on freshness/alerts without violating max concurrency.
- Failing collectors back off and recover safely.
- Profiling bursts capture high-resolution data linked to incidents.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for interval selection, backoff, and quarantine logic.
- Integration tests for scheduler respecting global limits.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_adaptive_polling.sh` and `tests/e2e/system/test_profile_burst.sh` with JSON summaries and logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T12:27:24.828417449Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T12:27:24.828386321Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.884931934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:13.660357995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-33h.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.2","depends_on_id":"bd-new.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:36.437869781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.3","title":"Implement incident replay + time‑travel views","description":"## Background
We already track incidents, alerts, and audit events, but there is no “time machine” view to replay what changed and why. Incident replay makes post‑mortems and triage dramatically faster.

## Goals
- Reconstruct fleet state at arbitrary timestamps (time‑travel queries).
- Provide an incident replay view in TUI + web + robot output.
- Link evidence to alerts, audit actions, and collector data.
- Allow export/share of replay snapshots for post‑mortems.

## Implementation
### Core Query Patterns
- Snapshot-at-time: latest snapshot <= T for each table.
- Context window: time range around incident for events (dcg, rano, mail, audit).
- Cache replay snapshots for fast scroll/scrub in UI.

### UI/CLI
- TUI: Incident replay screen with timeline scrubber.
- Web: `/api/incidents/:id/replay?at=...` returning structured JSON.
- CLI: `vc robot incident <id> --at <ts>`.
- Export: `vc incident export <id> --format json|md`.

### Tables
- Reuse `incident_*` tables + `audit_events` and `collector_health`.
- Add `incident_replay_snapshots(incident_id, ts, snapshot_json)` cache for fast replay.

## Risks
- Expensive queries across large tables; use caching and bounded windows.
- Ambiguity in “latest snapshot” if multiple collectors overlap.

## Success Criteria
- Incident replay reproduces health + alerts for known test incidents.
- Replay JSON is stable and documented.
- Exported snapshots can be shared without losing evidence context.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for time‑travel query correctness.
- Regression tests using fixture DuckDB snapshots.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_incident_replay.sh` with JSON summary and log artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:48.223462254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T11:18:55.329402753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T11:18:55.329378899Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:48.223462254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:14.386585958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2v3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:48.223462254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:40:26.440311623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:15.172827534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:48.223462254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:48.223462254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.3","depends_on_id":"bd-new.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:40:24.718858096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Operators need a compact daily/weekly digest that summarizes fleet health, alerts, usage, and progress without opening the UI. This is a low‑friction “ops heartbeat.”

- Generate Markdown + JSON digests for daily/weekly windows.
- Optionally deliver via MCP Agent Mail or webhook (reuse alert delivery channels where possible).
- Include health, alerts, usage, cost, and beads throughput.

- Top alerts + incident counts.
- Health score trends (overall + top 3 machines).
- Usage & rate‑limit forecast summary.
- Beads throughput + top blockers (bv snapshot).
- Notable events (dcg spikes, rano anomalies, pt findings).

- `vc report --window 24h --format md|json`.
- Schedule via cron/systemd or vc daemon tick.

### Tables
- `digest_reports(report_id, window, generated_at, summary_json, markdown)`.

## Risks
- Over‑verbose digests; must stay concise.
- Requires reliable data freshness indicators (bd-new.1).

## Success Criteria
- Digest is <1 page and highlights actionable items.
- Reports are reproducible for the same window.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for aggregation logic.
- Golden‑file tests for Markdown rendering.

 {"id":"bd-new.5","title":"Implement vc-node push agent (store‑and‑forward)","description":"## Background
Pull‑only works for small fleets, but as machine count grows it becomes expensive and brittle. A tiny vc‑node enables store‑and‑forward collection when remotes are offline and reduces SSH overhead.

## Goals
- Build a lightweight vc‑node binary that runs locally on remote machines.
- Collect locally, buffer artifacts, and push snapshots to vc‑hub on interval.
- Support offline backfill (store‑and‑forward) with deduplication.

## Implementation
### vc‑node
- Minimal config: hub URL/SSH target, machine_id, enabled collectors.
- Local spool directory for compressed JSONL/Parquet batches.
- Signed manifest per batch (hash + size + schema version).

### Hub Ingest
- Add `vc ingest --from <bundle>` to import snapshots.
- Deduplicate using (machine_id, collector, cursor, payload_hash).
- Record ingest audit events.

### Transfer Modes
- SSH push (scp/rsync) for MVP.
- Optional HTTPS upload later.

## Risks
- Artifact size growth if network is down; require max spool size.
- Duplicate ingestion if dedupe keys are weak.

## Success Criteria
- Remote machine can collect without continuous SSH polling.
- Backfilled bundles ingest cleanly with no duplicates.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for manifest hashing + dedupe keys.
- Integration tests for bundle ingest with synthetic fixtures.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/remote/test_vc_node_push.sh` with JSON summary + logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T12:44:34.324093797Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T12:44:34.324065174Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:17.770662983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:18.627128377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3nb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.5","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:09.567898497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-new.6","title":"Implement Prometheus exporter for vc metrics","description":"## Background
vc should integrate with existing observability stacks. A Prometheus exporter lets you build Grafana dashboards and alerts without custom tooling.

## Goals
- Expose `/metrics` endpoint with vc internal metrics and key fleet KPIs.
- Include collector freshness, alert counts, and health scores.
- Keep labels stable and documented.
- Provide a starter Grafana dashboard JSON.

## Implementation
### Metrics
- `vc_collector_freshness_seconds{machine,collector}`
- `vc_collector_success_total{machine,collector}`
- `vc_alerts_open_total{severity}`
- `vc_health_score{machine}`
- `vc_poll_duration_seconds{machine}`

### Server
- Add metrics router to vc_web or standalone metrics server.
- Use `prometheus` crate + `hyper` or axum.

### Grafana
- Ship a `docs/grafana/vc_overview.json` template referencing these metrics.

## Risks
- High-cardinality labels; must cap machine/collector label sets.

## Success Criteria
- Metrics scrape works locally and remotely.
- Grafana dashboard can be built from exposed metrics.

 {"id":"bd-new.7","title":"Implement GitHub issues/PR collector + correlation","description":"## Background
Repo status is more useful when correlated with issues/PRs and repo activity. This integrates GitHub metadata to show “what’s blocked” and “what’s waiting.”

## Goals
- Collect issues/PR counts, states, and labels per repo.
- Correlate with ru repo_id for dashboards and alerts.
- Cache results to avoid API rate limits.

## Implementation
### Integration
- Use `gh` CLI or GitHub REST API with token from config.
- Support org-wide + per-repo queries.
- Cache ETag/If‑Modified‑Since to reduce API calls.

### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE gh_repo_issue_pr_snapshot(
    repo_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    open_issues INTEGER,
    open_prs INTEGER,
    triage_json TEXT,
    label_breakdown_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (repo_id, collected_at)
);
```

## Risks
- API throttling; must respect rate limits.
- Repo mapping inconsistencies (path vs URL).

## Success Criteria
- Repo dashboard shows issue/PR counts and top labels.
- Collector respects GitHub rate limits.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for mapping repo_id to GH repo.
- Mock API tests for rate-limit handling.
